ringbuffer(3) Library Functions Manual ringbuffer(3)

NAME

ringbuffer - Ringbuffer

SYNOPSIS

Files


file ringbuffer.c
🎯 Lock-free circular buffer for audio streaming with atomic operations file ringbuffer.h
Lock-Free Ring Buffer and Frame Buffer Management.

Data Structures


struct ringbuffer_t
Lock-free ring buffer structure. struct audio_ring_buffer
Audio ring buffer for real-time audio streaming. struct frame_t
Frame structure that stores both data and actual size. struct multi_source_frame_t
Multi-source frame structure for multi-user support. struct framebuffer_t
Frame buffer structure for managing video frames.

Typedefs


typedef struct audio_ring_buffer audio_ring_buffer_t
Audio ring buffer for real-time audio streaming.

Functions


ringbuffer_t * ringbuffer_create (size_t element_size, size_t capacity)
Create a new ring buffer. void ringbuffer_destroy (ringbuffer_t *rb)
Destroy a ring buffer and free its memory. bool ringbuffer_write (ringbuffer_t *rb, const void *data)
Try to write an element to the ring buffer (non-blocking). bool ringbuffer_read (ringbuffer_t *rb, void *data)
Try to read an element from the ring buffer (non-blocking). bool ringbuffer_peek (ringbuffer_t *rb, void *data)
Peek at the next element without removing it. size_t ringbuffer_size (const ringbuffer_t *rb)
Get current number of elements in the buffer. bool ringbuffer_is_empty (const ringbuffer_t *rb)
Check if buffer is empty. bool ringbuffer_is_full (const ringbuffer_t *rb)
Check if buffer is full. void ringbuffer_clear (ringbuffer_t *rb)
Clear all elements from the buffer. framebuffer_t * framebuffer_create (size_t capacity)
Create a frame buffer for ASCII frames. framebuffer_t * framebuffer_create_multi (size_t capacity)
Create a multi-source frame buffer for multi-user support. void framebuffer_destroy (framebuffer_t *fb)
Destroy a frame buffer. bool framebuffer_write_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, const char *frame_data, size_t frame_size)
Write a frame to the buffer. bool framebuffer_read_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, frame_t *frame)
Read a frame from the buffer. void framebuffer_clear (framebuffer_t *fb)
Clear all frames from the buffer, freeing their data. bool framebuffer_write_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, const char *frame_data, size_t frame_size, uint32_t source_client_id, uint32_t frame_sequence, uint32_t timestamp)
Write a multi-source frame to the buffer (for multi-user support). bool framebuffer_read_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, multi_source_frame_t *frame)
Read a multi-source frame from the buffer. bool framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, multi_source_frame_t *frame)
Peek at the latest multi-source frame without removing it.

Audio Ring Buffer Constants


#define AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE 192000
Audio ring buffer size in samples (192000 samples = 4 seconds @ 48kHz). #define AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD 4800
Jitter buffer threshold - samples needed before starting playback. #define AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK 2400
Low water mark - warn when available drops below this. #define AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK (AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD * 2)
High water mark - drop OLD samples when buffer exceeds this. #define AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL (AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD + 480)
Target buffer level after dropping old samples. #define AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES 256
Crossfade duration in samples for smooth underrun recovery.

Frame Magic Numbers


#define FRAME_MAGIC MAGIC_FRAME_VALID
Magic number indicating a valid frame (0xDEADBEEF) - Alias for backward compatibility. #define FRAME_FREED MAGIC_FRAME_FREED
Magic number indicating a freed frame (0xFEEDFACE) - Alias for backward compatibility.

Detailed Description

This header provides lock-free ring buffer implementations for high-performance producer-consumer scenarios in ascii-chat. The system includes both generic lock-free ring buffers and specialized audio and frame buffers for media streaming.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Lock-free ring buffers using atomic operations
  • Thread-safe producer-consumer data transfer
  • Power-of-2 capacity optimization for fast modulo
  • Specialized audio ring buffers with jitter buffering
  • Frame buffer management for video frames
  • Multi-source frame support for multi-client scenarios

RING BUFFER ARCHITECTURE:

The lock-free ring buffer uses atomic operations for:

  • Lock-free writes (producer never blocks consumer)
  • Lock-free reads (consumer never blocks producer)
  • Atomic size tracking for fast emptiness checks
  • Power-of-2 capacity enables bit masking instead of modulo

AUDIO RING BUFFER:

Specialized audio ring buffer with:

  • Fixed-size circular buffer for audio samples
  • Jitter buffer threshold for network latency compensation
  • Mutex-protected operations for audio thread safety
  • Optimized for real-time audio streaming

FRAME BUFFER:

Frame buffer system for ASCII video frames:

  • Generic frame buffers for single-source scenarios
  • Multi-source frame buffers for multi-client support
  • Magic number validation for corruption detection
  • Automatic frame data management

PERFORMANCE BENEFITS:

  • Lock-free operations minimize latency
  • Power-of-2 capacity enables fast bit masking
  • Zero-allocation pattern after initialization
  • Efficient producer-consumer data transfer

THREAD SAFETY:

  • Generic ring buffers: Lock-free using atomic operations
  • Audio ring buffers: Mutex-protected for thread safety
  • Frame buffers: Mutex-protected for concurrent access

Note

Ring buffers are ideal for producer-consumer scenarios where one thread produces data and another consumes it.

The lock-free ring buffer requires power-of-2 capacity for optimal performance (uses bit masking instead of modulo).

Audio ring buffers use jitter buffering to compensate for network latency and packet timing variations.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

August 2025

Macro Definition Documentation

#define AUDIO_CROSSFADE_SAMPLES 256

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Crossfade duration in samples for smooth underrun recovery. When recovering from an underrun, we fade in the audio over this many samples to prevent clicks and pops. 256 samples @ 48kHz = ~5.3ms

Definition at line 186 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by audio_ring_buffer_read().

#define AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD 4800

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Jitter buffer threshold - samples needed before starting playback. This threshold determines how much audio must be buffered before playback starts. Too small = underruns and audio gaps when network packets arrive late Too large = excessive latency + AEC3 gets silence during fill period

INCREASED from 960 (20ms) to 4800 (100ms) to eliminate stuttering caused by:

  • Network jitter (packets arriving in bursts)
  • Packet processing overhead
  • Thread scheduling delays

4800 samples @ 48kHz = 100ms = 5 Opus frames

Definition at line 143 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by audio_ring_buffer_read().

#define AUDIO_JITTER_HIGH_WATER_MARK (AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD * 2)

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

High water mark - drop OLD samples when buffer exceeds this. When the playback buffer grows beyond this level, we have accumulated too much latency. We drop OLD samples to keep latency bounded. This prevents the "latency grows over time" problem where network bursts fill the buffer faster than playback can drain it.

Set to 2x jitter threshold = 40ms for LAN. This gives enough headroom for network jitter while preventing unbounded latency accumulation. 1920 samples @ 48kHz = 40ms = 2 Opus frames

Definition at line 169 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by audio_ring_buffer_write().

#define AUDIO_JITTER_LOW_WATER_MARK 2400

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Low water mark - warn when available drops below this. When playback buffer drops below this threshold, we log a warning but keep playing to avoid choppy audio. The jitter buffer should absorb network jitter without constantly pausing/resuming.

INCREASED from 480 (10ms) to 2400 (50ms) to provide adequate headroom and prevent thousands of micro-underruns that cause audio stuttering.

2400 samples @ 48kHz = 50ms = 2.5 Opus frames

Definition at line 156 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by audio_ring_buffer_read().

#define AUDIO_JITTER_TARGET_LEVEL (AUDIO_JITTER_BUFFER_THRESHOLD + 480)

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Target buffer level after dropping old samples. When we drop old samples due to exceeding HIGH_WATER_MARK, we bring the buffer back down to this level. Set slightly above jitter threshold to avoid immediately triggering underrun detection.

1440 samples @ 48kHz = 30ms = 1.5 Opus frames

Definition at line 179 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by audio_ring_buffer_write().

#define AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE 192000

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Audio ring buffer size in samples (192000 samples = 4 seconds @ 48kHz). Sized to handle:

1.
Single-client period where audio accumulates before a second client joins
2.
Mixer processing overhead (can take 3-15ms, exceeding 10ms target)
3.
System scheduling delays under load

4 seconds provides headroom for mixer slowdowns and prevents overflow during processing spikes. The mixer reads adaptively to catch up.

Definition at line 128 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by audio_ring_buffer_available_read(), audio_ring_buffer_available_write(), audio_ring_buffer_peek(), audio_ring_buffer_read(), audio_ring_buffer_write(), and session_display_write_audio().

#define FRAME_FREED MAGIC_FRAME_FREED

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Magic number indicating a freed frame (0xFEEDFACE) - Alias for backward compatibility.

Definition at line 420 of file ringbuffer.h.

#define FRAME_MAGIC MAGIC_FRAME_VALID

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Magic number indicating a valid frame (0xDEADBEEF) - Alias for backward compatibility.

Definition at line 418 of file ringbuffer.h.

Referenced by framebuffer_clear(), framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame(), framebuffer_read_frame(), framebuffer_read_multi_frame(), and framebuffer_write_frame().

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct audio_ring_buffer audio_ring_buffer_t

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Audio ring buffer for real-time audio streaming. Specialized ring buffer for audio samples with jitter buffering to compensate for network latency and packet timing variations. Uses mutex-protected operations for thread-safe audio access.

Features:

  • Jitter buffering: waits for threshold fill before starting playback
  • Low water mark: resets jitter state when buffer runs low
  • Crossfade: smoothly fades audio in/out during underrun recovery
  • Underrun tracking: counts underruns for diagnostics

Function Documentation

void framebuffer_clear (framebuffer_t * fb)

#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>

Clear all frames from the buffer, freeing their data.

Parameters

fb Frame buffer (must not be NULL)

Removes all frames from the buffer and frees their associated data. This operation is thread-safe but should be used with caution if other threads may be reading frames.

Note

Thread-safe: Protected by mutex for concurrent access.

Definition at line 322 of file ringbuffer.c.

322                                           {
323   if (!fb || !fb->rb)
324     return;
325 
326   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer (was missing, causing race conditions)
327   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
328 
329   // Check the element size to determine frame type
330   if (fb->rb->element_size == sizeof(multi_source_frame_t)) {
331     // Multi-source frame buffer - read and free multi_source_frame_t
332     multi_source_frame_t multi_frame;
333     while (ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, &multi_frame)) {
334       if (IS_FRAME_VALID((frame_t *)&multi_frame)) {
335         MARK_FRAME_FREED((frame_t *)&multi_frame); // Mark as freed to detect use-after-free
336         buffer_pool_free(NULL, multi_frame.data, multi_frame.size);
337       } else if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(multi_frame.magic, FRAME_MAGIC) && multi_frame.magic != 0) {
338         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame magic 0x%x during clear",
339                   multi_frame.magic);
340       }
341     }
342   } else if (fb->rb->element_size == sizeof(frame_t)) {
343     // Single-source frame buffer - read and free frame_t
344     frame_t frame;
345     while (ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, &frame)) {
346       if (IS_FRAME_VALID(&frame)) {
347         MARK_FRAME_FREED(&frame); // Mark as freed to detect use-after-free
348         buffer_pool_free(NULL, frame.data, frame.size);
349       } else if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame.magic, FRAME_MAGIC) && frame.magic != 0) {
350         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid frame magic 0x%x during clear", frame.magic);
351       }
352     }
353   } else {
354     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Unknown frame buffer type with element size %zu", fb->rb->element_size);
355   }
356 
357   // Clear the ringbuffer indices
358   ringbuffer_clear(fb->rb);
359 
360   // Zero out the entire buffer to prevent any dangling pointers
361   if (fb->rb->buffer) {
362     // Check for integer overflow before multiplication
363     if (fb->rb->capacity > SIZE_MAX / fb->rb->element_size) {
364       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size would overflow: capacity=%zu, element_size=%zu", fb->rb->capacity,
365                 fb->rb->element_size);
366       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
367       return;
368     }
369     size_t buffer_size = fb->rb->capacity * fb->rb->element_size;
370     SAFE_MEMSET(fb->rb->buffer, buffer_size, 0, buffer_size);
371   }
372 
373   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
374 }




References ringbuffer_t::buffer, buffer_pool_free(),
    buffer_size, ringbuffer_t::capacity, frame_t::data,
    ringbuffer_t::element_size, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, frame, FRAME_MAGIC,
    IS_FRAME_VALID, IS_MAGIC_VALID, frame_t::magic,
    MARK_FRAME_FREED, framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_clear(),
    ringbuffer_read(), SAFE_MEMSET, SET_ERRNO, and
    frame_t::size.


Referenced by framebuffer_destroy().






framebuffer_t * framebuffer_create (size_t capacity)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Create a frame buffer for ASCII frames.


Parameters


capacity Number of frames to buffer


Returns


Frame buffer or NULL on failure


Creates a new frame buffer for single-source frame scenarios. The
    capacity is automatically rounded up to the next power of 2 for optimal
    performance.


Definition at line 146 of file ringbuffer.c.


146                                                    {
147   if (capacity == 0) {
148     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid frame buffer parameters");
149     return NULL;
150   }
151 
152   framebuffer_t *fb;
153   fb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(framebuffer_t), framebuffer_t *);
154 
155   // Initialize mutex for thread-safe access
156   if (mutex_init(&fb->mutex, "framebuffer") != 0) {
157     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize framebuffer mutex");
158     SAFE_FREE(fb);
159     return NULL;
160   }
161 
162   // Create ringbuffer to store frame_t structs
163   fb->rb = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(frame_t), capacity);
164   if (!fb->rb) {
165     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame buffer");
166     mutex_destroy(&fb->mutex);
167     SAFE_FREE(fb);
168     return NULL;
169   }
170 
171   return fb;
172 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_THREAD, framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_create(),
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, and SET_ERRNO.






framebuffer_t * framebuffer_create_multi (size_t
  capacity)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Create a multi-source frame buffer for multi-user support.


Parameters


capacity Number of frames to buffer


Returns


Frame buffer or NULL on failure


Creates a new frame buffer for multi-client frame scenarios.
    Supports tracking frame sources, sequence numbers, and timestamps for proper
    multi-user frame display.


Definition at line 174 of file ringbuffer.c.


174                                                          {
175   if (capacity == 0) {
176     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid capacity: %zu", capacity);
177     return NULL;
178   }
179 
180   framebuffer_t *fb;
181   fb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(framebuffer_t), framebuffer_t *);
182 
183   // Initialize mutex for thread-safe access
184   if (mutex_init(&fb->mutex, "framebuffer_multi") != 0) {
185     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize framebuffer mutex");
186     SAFE_FREE(fb);
187     return NULL;
188   }
189 
190   // Create ringbuffer to store multi_source_frame_t structs
191   fb->rb = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(multi_source_frame_t), capacity);
192 
193   if (!fb->rb) {
194     mutex_destroy(&fb->mutex);
195     SAFE_FREE(fb);
196     return NULL;
197   }
198 
199   return fb;
200 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_THREAD,
    framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_create(), SAFE_CALLOC,
    SAFE_FREE, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  framebuffer_destroy (framebuffer_t * fb)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Destroy a frame buffer.


Parameters


fb Frame buffer to destroy (must not be
  NULL)


Frees all memory associated with the frame buffer, including any
    frames still in the buffer. The buffer must not be in use by any threads
    when this function is called.


Definition at line 202 of file ringbuffer.c.


202                                             {
203   if (!fb)
204     return;
205 
206   // Add magic number check to detect double-free using rb pointer
207   if (fb->rb == (ringbuffer_t *)0xDEADBEEF) {
208     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "DOUBLE-FREE DETECTED: framebuffer %p already destroyed!", fb);
209     return;
210   }
211 
212   framebuffer_clear(fb);
213   ringbuffer_destroy(fb->rb);
214   mutex_destroy(&fb->mutex);
215 
216   // Mark as destroyed before freeing
217   fb->rb = (ringbuffer_t *)0xDEADBEEF;
218   SAFE_FREE(fb);
219 }






References ERROR_INVALID_STATE, framebuffer_clear(),
    framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_destroy(),
    framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_FREE, and
    SET_ERRNO.






bool framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame
  (framebuffer_t * fb, multi_source_frame_t * frame)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Peek at the latest multi-source frame without removing it.


Parameters


fb Frame buffer (must not be NULL)


frame Pointer to multi_source_frame_t
  struct to be filled (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is empty


Examines the latest multi-source frame without removing it from
    the buffer. Useful for previewing frames or checking frame metadata before
    reading.




Note


Thread-safe: Protected by mutex for concurrent access.



The frame data pointer is valid only until the buffer is modified.
    Do not use the data pointer after subsequent buffer operations.




Definition at line 459 of file ringbuffer.c.


459                                                                                          {
460   if (!fb || !fb->rb || !frame) {
461     return false;
462   }
463 
464   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer
465   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
466 
467   // Use ringbuffer_peek to get the frame without consuming it
468   bool result = ringbuffer_peek(fb->rb, frame);
469 
470   if (result) {
471     // Validate frame magic
472     if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
473       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame magic 0x%x (expected 0x%x) in peek",
474                 frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC);
475       frame->data = NULL;
476       frame->size = 0;
477       memset(frame->source_client_id, 0, sizeof(frame->source_client_id));
478       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
479       return false;
480     }
481 
482     // Additional validation
483     if (frame->size == 0 || !frame->data) {
484       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame data (size=%zu, data=%p) in peek",
485                 frame->size, frame->data);
486       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
487       return false;
488     }
489 
490     // IMPORTANT: We need to make a copy of the data since we're not consuming the frame
491     // The original data pointer will remain valid in the ring buffer
492     // Caller is responsible for freeing this copy with buffer_pool_free()
493     char *data_copy;
494     data_copy = (char *)buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, frame->size);
495     if (!data_copy) {
496       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for frame data copy in peek");
497       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
498       return false;
499     }
500     SAFE_MEMCPY(data_copy, frame->size, frame->data, frame->size);
501     frame->data = data_copy;
502   }
503 
504   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
505   return result;
506 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_MEMORY, frame, FRAME_MAGIC,
    IS_MAGIC_VALID, framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_peek(),
    SAFE_MEMCPY, and SET_ERRNO.






bool framebuffer_read_frame (framebuffer_t * fb,
  frame_t * frame)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Read a frame from the buffer.


Parameters


fb Frame buffer (must not be NULL)


frame Pointer to frame_t struct to be
  filled (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is empty


Reads a single-source frame from the buffer. The frame data
    pointer points to data owned by the buffer until the frame is cleared or the
    buffer is destroyed.




Note


Thread-safe: Protected by mutex for concurrent access.



The caller must not free the frame data pointer - it's owned by
    the buffer until the frame is consumed.




Definition at line 275 of file ringbuffer.c.


275                                                                {
276   if (!fb || !frame) {
277     return false;
278   }
279 
280   // Initialize frame to safe values
281   frame->magic = 0;
282   frame->data = NULL;
283   frame->size = 0;
284 
285   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer (fixes TOCTOU race condition)
286   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
287 
288   bool result = ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, frame);
289 
290   // Validate the frame we just read
291   if (result) {
292     if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
293       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid frame magic 0x%x (expected 0x%x)", frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC);
294       frame->data = NULL;
295       frame->size = 0;
296       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
297       return false;
298     }
299 
300     if (IS_FRAME_FREED(frame)) {
301       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Reading already-freed frame!");
302       frame->data = NULL;
303       frame->size = 0;
304       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
305       return false;
306     }
307 
308     if (frame->size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
309       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Frame size too large: %zu", frame->size);
310       SAFE_FREE(frame->data);
311       frame->data = NULL;
312       frame->size = 0;
313       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
314       return false;
315     }
316   }
317 
318   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
319   return result;
320 }






References ERROR_INVALID_STATE, frame,
    FRAME_MAGIC, IS_FRAME_FREED, IS_MAGIC_VALID,
    framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_read(), SAFE_FREE, and
    SET_ERRNO.






bool framebuffer_read_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *
  fb, multi_source_frame_t * frame)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Read a multi-source frame from the buffer.


Parameters


fb Frame buffer (must not be NULL)


frame Pointer to multi_source_frame_t
  struct to be filled (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is empty


Reads a multi-source frame from the buffer along with its
    metadata. The frame data pointer points to data owned by the buffer until
    the frame is cleared or the buffer is destroyed.




Note


Thread-safe: Protected by mutex for concurrent access.



The caller must not free the frame data pointer - it's owned by
    the buffer until the frame is consumed.




Definition at line 424 of file ringbuffer.c.


424                                                                                   {
425   if (!fb || !fb->rb || !frame) {
426     return false;
427   }
428 
429   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer
430   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
431 
432   bool result = ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, frame);
433 
434   if (result) {
435     // Validate frame magic
436     if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
437       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame magic 0x%x (expected 0x%x)", frame->magic,
438                 FRAME_MAGIC);
439       frame->data = NULL;
440       frame->size = 0;
441       memset(frame->source_client_id, 0, sizeof(frame->source_client_id));
442       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
443       return false;
444     }
445 
446     // Additional validation
447     if (frame->size == 0 || !frame->data) {
448       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame data (size=%zu, data=%p)", frame->size,
449                 frame->data);
450       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
451       return false;
452     }
453   }
454 
455   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
456   return result;
457 }






References ERROR_INVALID_STATE, frame,
    FRAME_MAGIC, IS_MAGIC_VALID, framebuffer_t::mutex,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, framebuffer_t::rb,
    ringbuffer_read(), and SET_ERRNO.






bool framebuffer_write_frame (framebuffer_t * fb,
  const char * frame_data, size_t frame_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Write a frame to the buffer.


Parameters


fb Frame buffer (must not be NULL)


frame_data Frame data (must not be NULL)


frame_size Actual size of frame data in bytes


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is full


Writes a single-source frame to the buffer. The frame data is
    copied into the buffer, so the caller retains ownership of the original
    data.




Note


Thread-safe: Protected by mutex for concurrent
  access.


Definition at line 221 of file ringbuffer.c.


221                                                                                            {
222   if (!fb || !frame_data || frame_size == 0)
223     return false;
224 
225   // Validate frame size to prevent overflow
226   if (frame_size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) { // 10MB max for ASCII frames
227     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Rejecting oversized frame: %zu bytes", frame_size);
228     return false;
229   }
230 
231   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer (was missing, causing race conditions)
232   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
233 
234   // Check if buffer is full - if so, we need to drop the oldest frame
235   if (ringbuffer_size(fb->rb) >= fb->rb->capacity) {
236     // Buffer is full, read and free the oldest frame before writing new one
237     frame_t old_frame;
238     if (ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, &old_frame)) {
239       if (IS_FRAME_VALID(&old_frame)) {
240         MARK_FRAME_FREED(&old_frame);
241         // Use buffer_pool_free since data was allocated with buffer_pool_alloc
242         buffer_pool_free(NULL, old_frame.data, old_frame.size);
243       } else if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(old_frame.magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
244         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid old frame magic 0x%x when dropping", old_frame.magic);
245       }
246     }
247   }
248 
249   // Allocate a copy of the frame data using buffer pool for better performance
250   char *frame_copy = (char *)buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, frame_size + 1);
251   if (!frame_copy) {
252     mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
253     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes from buffer pool for frame", frame_size + 1);
254     return false;
255   }
256 
257   SAFE_MEMCPY(frame_copy, frame_size, frame_data, frame_size);
258   frame_copy[frame_size] = '\0'; // Ensure null termination
259 
260   // Create a frame_t struct with the copy (store allocated size for proper cleanup)
261   frame_t frame = {.magic = MAGIC_FRAME_VALID, .size = frame_size + 1, .data = frame_copy};
262 
263   bool result = ringbuffer_write(fb->rb, &frame);
264 
265   if (!result) {
266     // If we still couldn't write to ringbuffer, return the buffer to pool
267     buffer_pool_free(NULL, frame_copy, frame_size + 1);
268     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to write frame to ringbuffer even after dropping oldest");
269   }
270 
271   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
272   return result;
273 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), buffer_pool_free(),
    ringbuffer_t::capacity, frame_t::data,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    frame, FRAME_MAGIC, IS_FRAME_VALID,
    IS_MAGIC_VALID, frame_t::magic, MAGIC_FRAME_VALID,
    MARK_FRAME_FREED, framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_read(),
    ringbuffer_size(), ringbuffer_write(), SAFE_MEMCPY,
    SET_ERRNO, and frame_t::size.






bool framebuffer_write_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *
  fb, const char * frame_data, size_t frame_size, uint32_t
  source_client_id, uint32_t frame_sequence, uint32_t
  timestamp)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Write a multi-source frame to the buffer (for multi-user
  support).


Parameters


fb Frame buffer (must not be NULL)


frame_data Frame data (must not be NULL)


frame_size Actual size of frame data in bytes


source_client_id Client ID that sent this frame


frame_sequence Frame sequence number for ordering


timestamp Timestamp when frame was captured


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is full


Writes a multi-source frame to the buffer with associated
    metadata. The frame data is copied into the buffer, so the caller retains
    ownership of the original data.




Note


Thread-safe: Protected by mutex for concurrent access.



The frame buffer must have been created with
    framebuffer_create_multi() for multi-source support.




Definition at line 378 of file ringbuffer.c.


379                                                                                                            {
380   if (!fb || !fb->rb || !frame_data || frame_size == 0) {
381     return false;
382   }
383 
384   // Allocate memory for frame data using buffer pool for better performance
385   char *data_copy = (char *)buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, frame_size);
386   if (!data_copy) {
387     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes from buffer pool for multi-source frame", frame_size);
388     return false;
389   }
390 
391   // Copy frame data
392   SAFE_MEMCPY(data_copy, frame_size, frame_data, frame_size);
393 
394   // Convert client ID number to string for storage in frame metadata
395   char client_id_str[64];
396   snprintf(client_id_str, sizeof(client_id_str), "%u", source_client_id);
397 
398   // Create multi-source frame
399   multi_source_frame_t multi_frame = {
400       .magic = MAGIC_FRAME_VALID,
401       .source_client_id = {0},
402       .frame_sequence = frame_sequence,
403       .timestamp = timestamp,
404       .size = frame_size,
405       .data = data_copy,
406   };
407   SAFE_MEMCPY(multi_frame.source_client_id, sizeof(multi_frame.source_client_id), client_id_str, strlen(client_id_str) + 1);
408 
409   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer
410   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
411 
412   // Try to write to ring buffer
413   bool success = ringbuffer_write(fb->rb, &multi_frame);
414   if (!success) {
415     // Buffer full, return the buffer to pool
416     buffer_pool_free(NULL, data_copy, frame_size);
417     log_debug("Frame buffer full, dropping multi-source frame from client %u", source_client_id);
418   }
419 
420   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
421   return success;
422 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), buffer_pool_free(),
    ERROR_MEMORY, log_debug, MAGIC_FRAME_VALID,
    framebuffer_t::mutex, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    framebuffer_t::rb, ringbuffer_write(), SAFE_MEMCPY,
    SET_ERRNO, and multi_source_frame_t::source_client_id.






void
  ringbuffer_clear (ringbuffer_t * rb)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Clear all elements from the buffer.


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


Removes all elements from the buffer and resets it to empty state.
    This operation is not atomic with respect to concurrent read/write
    operations - use with caution in multi-threaded scenarios.




Note


Thread-safe: Uses atomic operations, but concurrent
  operations may still occur during clearing.


Definition at line 133 of file ringbuffer.c.


133                                         {
134   if (rb) {
135     atomic_store_u64(&rb->head, 0);
136     atomic_store_u64(&rb->tail, 0);
137     atomic_store_u64(&rb->size, 0);
138   }
139 }






References atomic_store_u64(), ringbuffer_t::head,
    ringbuffer_t::size, and ringbuffer_t::tail.


Referenced by framebuffer_clear().






ringbuffer_t * ringbuffer_create (size_t element_size,
  size_t capacity)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Create a new ring buffer.


Parameters


element_size Size of each element in bytes


capacity Number of elements (will be rounded up to power of 2)


Returns


Pointer to ring buffer or NULL on failure


Creates a new lock-free ring buffer with the specified element
    size and capacity. The capacity is automatically rounded up to the next
    power of 2 to enable fast bit masking optimizations.


Definition at line 29 of file ringbuffer.c.


29                                                                       {
30   if (element_size == 0 || capacity == 0) {
31     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid ring buffer parameters: element_size=%zu, capacity=%zu", element_size,
32               capacity);
33     return NULL;
34   }
35 
36   ringbuffer_t *rb;
37   rb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(ringbuffer_t), ringbuffer_t *);
38 
39   /* Round capacity up to power of 2 for optimization */
40   size_t actual_capacity = math_next_power_of_two(capacity);
41 
42   rb->buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(actual_capacity, element_size, char *);
43 
44   rb->element_size = element_size;
45   rb->capacity = actual_capacity;
46   rb->is_power_of_two = true;
47   rb->capacity_mask = actual_capacity - 1;
48   /* Atomic fields are zero-initialized by SAFE_CALLOC */
49 
50   return rb;
51 }






References ringbuffer_t::buffer,
    ringbuffer_t::capacity, ringbuffer_t::capacity_mask,
    ringbuffer_t::element_size, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ringbuffer_t::is_power_of_two, SAFE_CALLOC, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acip_webrtc_transport_create(),
    acip_websocket_client_transport_create(),
    acip_websocket_server_transport_create(),
    framebuffer_create(), framebuffer_create_multi(),
    session_host_add_client(), and
    session_host_add_memory_participant().






void
  ringbuffer_destroy (ringbuffer_t * rb)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Destroy a ring buffer and free its memory.


Parameters


rb Ring buffer to destroy (must not be NULL)


Frees all memory associated with the ring buffer. The buffer must
    not be in use by any threads when this function is called.


Definition at line 53 of file ringbuffer.c.


53                                           {
54   if (rb) {
55     SAFE_FREE(rb->buffer);
56     SAFE_FREE(rb);
57   }
58 }

Referenced by acip_webrtc_transport_create(),
    acip_websocket_client_transport_create(),
    acip_websocket_server_transport_create(),

    session_host_add_memory_participant(), session_host_destroy(),
    and session_host_remove_client().






bool ringbuffer_is_empty (const ringbuffer_t *
  rb)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Check if buffer is empty.


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if buffer is empty, false otherwise


Performs a fast check to determine if the buffer contains any
    elements. More efficient than checking if size() == 0.




Note


Thread-safe: Uses atomic operations for lock-free
  check.


Definition at line 125 of file ringbuffer.c.


125                                                  {
126   return ringbuffer_size(rb) == 0;
127 }






References ringbuffer_size().






bool ringbuffer_is_full (const ringbuffer_t *
  rb)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Check if buffer is full.


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if buffer is full, false otherwise


Performs a fast check to determine if the buffer has reached
    capacity. More efficient than checking if size() == capacity.




Note


Thread-safe: Uses atomic operations for lock-free
  check.


Definition at line 129 of file ringbuffer.c.


129                                                 {
130   return rb ? ringbuffer_size(rb) >= rb->capacity : true;
131 }






References ringbuffer_t::capacity, and
    ringbuffer_size().






bool ringbuffer_peek (ringbuffer_t * rb, void *
  data)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Peek at the next element without removing it.


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


data Buffer to peek into (must not be NULL, must be element_size
  bytes)


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is empty


Examines the next element without removing it from the buffer.
    This is useful for previewing data before committing to reading it.




Note


SPSC only: Single Producer, Single Consumer. NOT
  thread-safe for multiple concurrent peek operations.


Definition at line 104 of file ringbuffer.c.


104                                                    {
105   if (!rb || !data)
106     return false;
107 
108   size_t current_size = atomic_load_u64(&rb->size);
109   if (current_size == 0) {
110     return false; /* Buffer empty */
111   }
112 
113   size_t tail = atomic_load_u64(&rb->tail);
114 
115   /* Copy data without updating tail */
116   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, rb->element_size, rb->buffer + (tail * rb->element_size), rb->element_size);
117 
118   return true;
119 }






References atomic_load_u64(), ringbuffer_t::buffer,
    ringbuffer_t::element_size, SAFE_MEMCPY,
    ringbuffer_t::size, and ringbuffer_t::tail.


Referenced by framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame().






bool ringbuffer_read (ringbuffer_t * rb, void *
  data)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Try to read an element from the ring buffer (non-blocking).


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


data Buffer to read into (must not be NULL, must be element_size
  bytes)


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is empty


Attempts to read an element from the ring buffer. This is a
    non-blocking operation that uses lock-free atomic operations. Returns false
    immediately if the buffer is empty.




Note


SPSC only: Single Producer, Single Consumer. NOT
  thread-safe for multiple consumers - use external synchronization if
  needed.


Definition at line 82 of file ringbuffer.c.


82                                                    {
83   if (!rb || !data)
84     return false;
85 
86   size_t current_size = atomic_load_u64(&rb->size);
87   if (current_size == 0) {
88     return false; /* Buffer empty */
89   }
90 
91   size_t tail = atomic_load_u64(&rb->tail);
92   size_t next_tail = (tail + 1) & rb->capacity_mask;
93 
94   /* Copy data */
95   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, rb->element_size, rb->buffer + (tail * rb->element_size), rb->element_size);
96 
97   /* Update tail and size atomically */
98   atomic_store_u64(&rb->tail, next_tail);
99   atomic_fetch_sub_u64(&rb->size, 1);
100 
101   return true;
102 }






References atomic_fetch_sub_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    ringbuffer_t::buffer, ringbuffer_t::capacity_mask,
    ringbuffer_t::element_size, SAFE_MEMCPY,
    ringbuffer_t::size, and ringbuffer_t::tail.


Referenced by framebuffer_clear(),
    framebuffer_read_frame(), framebuffer_read_multi_frame(), and
    framebuffer_write_frame().






size_t
  ringbuffer_size (const ringbuffer_t * rb)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Get current number of elements in the buffer.


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


Returns


Number of elements currently in the buffer


Returns the current size of the buffer. This is a snapshot value
    that may change immediately after return due to concurrent operations.




Note


Thread-safe: Uses atomic operations for lock-free size
  check.


Definition at line 121 of file ringbuffer.c.


121                                                {
122   return rb ? atomic_load_u64(&rb->size) : 0;
123 }






References atomic_load_u64(), and
    ringbuffer_t::size.


Referenced by framebuffer_write_frame(),
    ringbuffer_is_empty(), and ringbuffer_is_full().






bool ringbuffer_write (ringbuffer_t * rb, const
  void * data)


#include <include/ascii-chat/ringbuffer.h>


Try to write an element to the ring buffer (non-blocking).


Parameters


rb Ring buffer (must not be NULL)


data Data to write (must not be NULL, must be element_size bytes)


Returns


true if successful, false if buffer is full


Attempts to write an element to the ring buffer. This is a
    non-blocking operation that uses lock-free atomic operations. Returns false
    immediately if the buffer is full.




Note


SPSC only: Single Producer, Single Consumer. NOT
  thread-safe for multiple producers - use external synchronization if
  needed.


Definition at line 60 of file ringbuffer.c.


60                                                           {
61   if (!rb || !data)
62     return false;
63 
64   size_t current_size = atomic_load_u64(&rb->size);
65   if (current_size >= rb->capacity) {
66     return false; /* Buffer full */
67   }
68 
69   size_t head = atomic_load_u64(&rb->head);
70   size_t next_head = (head + 1) & rb->capacity_mask;
71 
72   /* Copy data */
73   SAFE_MEMCPY(rb->buffer + (head * rb->element_size), rb->element_size, data, rb->element_size);
74 
75   /* Update head and size atomically */
76   atomic_store_u64(&rb->head, next_head);
77   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&rb->size, 1);
78 
79   return true;
80 }






References atomic_fetch_add_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), atomic_store_u64(),
    ringbuffer_t::buffer, ringbuffer_t::capacity,
    ringbuffer_t::capacity_mask, ringbuffer_t::element_size,
    ringbuffer_t::head, SAFE_MEMCPY, and
    ringbuffer_t::size.


Referenced by framebuffer_write_frame(),
    framebuffer_write_multi_frame(), and
    session_host_inject_audio().
